Question:
                                What is the total number of lunch meals I got from the chicken fajitas and lentil soup?

                                Question date:
                                2023/05/28 (Sun) 13:45

                                Noisy expected-answer label:
                                8 meals

                                Retrieved memory context:
                                            MEMORY-ONLY CONTEXT
            This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
            The retrieved memory below was selected for the immediately following user request.
            Treat it as the strongest available remembered context, stronger than guesses, defaults, or general world knowledge.
            Use selected active memory for the answer unless it is clearly irrelevant, insufficient, stale, internally conflicting, or contradicted by the current user message.
            Do not claim that raw sources are verified facts; prefer active claims for facts, notes for rationale, and action items for commitments.
            If raw source wording conflicts with active typed memory, trust the active typed memory for current facts.
            
            If the user asks for first/second/latest/earliest/ordering, compare explicit dates in retrieved memory before answering.
            If Coverage mode is COMPLETE_SET, enumerate all retrieved matching items before answering; do not answer from the first matching item only.
            If the user asks for an exact quote, exact wording, source, or when something was said, prefer the complete source text from Retrieved evidence; evidence quote fields are short excerpts and may be incomplete.
            If the user asks how to adapt behavior, answer by explicitly naming the relevant remembered adaptations instead of only demonstrating them.

            Namespace: benchmark:longmemeval:8979f9ec
            Context mode: FACTUAL
            Coverage mode: COMPLETE_SET
            Require evidence fallback: true

            Retrieved profile:
            none

            Retrieved action items:
            none

            Retrieved claims:
            - claim hot-path-e6d35259a759-claim-6 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user wants hearty soup recipes that are good for meal prep.; scope=User food and recipe preferences across the namespace.; context="After making a big batch of lentil soup that lasted five lunches, the user asked for other hearty soup recipes suitable for meal prep."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:1811e88a116509b2f2cf6865fe348b48 quote="Do you have any other hearty soup recipes that would be good for meal prep?"
- claim hot-path-e6d35259a759-claim-5 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user has experience making lentil soup in a big batch that lasted for 5 lunches.; scope=User's cooking and meal-prep experience with lentil soup.; context="The user cited lentil soup as a successful meal-prep example when asking for similar hearty soup recipes."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:1811e88a116509b2f2cf6865fe348b48 quote="I just made a big batch of lentil soup that lasted me for 5 lunches"
- claim hot-path-15a69a96e0a9-claim-7 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user has experience making and eating chicken fajitas with sautéed onions and bell peppers.; scope=User's cooking and meal history involving chicken fajitas.; context="The user said they made chicken fajitas the previous week and finished the leftovers for lunch."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:ed5acc74c75736896a6524720160a12b quote="my chicken fajitas with sautéed onions and bell peppers that I made last week"
- claim hot-path-e6d35259a759-claim-4 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user wants new lunch recipes that are suitable for meal prep.; scope=User food and recipe preferences across the namespace.; context="In a past recipe-planning chat, the user asked for new recipes to try, particularly for lunch, while noting they had been meal prepping a lot."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:1811e88a116509b2f2cf6865fe348b48 quote="I'm looking for some new recipes to try out, particularly for lunch."
- claim hot-path-e6d35259a759-claim-8 [ACTIVE] prefers family=prefers: The user likes the idea of Butternut Squash and Chickpea Curry Soup having a creamy texture and Indian spices.; scope=User food flavor and texture preferences in recipe selection.; context="The user explained why Butternut Squash and Chickpea Curry Soup appealed to them."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:1811e88a116509b2f2cf6865fe348b48 quote="I love the idea of the creamy texture and the Indian spices"
- claim hot-path-e6d35259a759-claim-13 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user wants suggestions for protein sources or vegetables to add to cauliflower rice.; scope=User cauliflower-rice recipe goals.; context="The user asked for ways to add proteins and vegetables to cauliflower rice while discussing meal prep and low-carb eating."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:1811e88a116509b2f2cf6865fe348b48 quote="Do you have any suggestions for other protein sources or vegetables that I could add to my cauliflower rice?"

            Retrieved notes:
            - note hot-path-e6d35259a759-note-2 [CONTEXT/ACTIVE]: Meal-prep lunch pattern: hearty soups and cauliflower rice bowls; The user is currently using meal prep to save time, especially for lunches, and gravitates toward batch-friendly hearty soups and customizable cauliflower rice meals. A successful reference point is a big batch of lentil soup that lasted for 5 lunches, while cauliflower rice is used often as a low-carb base with varied seasonings, proteins, and vegetables.; scope=User's lunch meal-prep habits and reusable recipe-selection context from LongMemEval 8979f9ec session 2; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:1811e88a116509b2f2cf6865fe348b48 quote="I've been meal prepping a lot lately and it's been a huge time saver."
- note hot-path-15a69a96e0a9-note-2 [CONTEXT/ACTIVE]: Leftover-vegetable cooking direction focused on food waste reduction; The user is looking for creative recipe ideas for using up leftover vegetables because they are trying to reduce food waste. They value practical leftover strategies and noted getting three meals from homemade chicken fajitas with sautéed onions and bell peppers.; scope=User's cooking and meal-planning context around leftover vegetables and food waste reduction; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:ed5acc74c75736896a6524720160a12b quote="I've been trying to reduce food waste and I want to get creative with what I have on hand."

LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2023/05/25 (Thu) 02:39
Transcript:
user: I'm looking for some new recipes to try out, particularly for lunch. I've been meal prepping a lot lately and it's been a huge time saver.
assistant: Meal prep is a game-changer, isn't it? It's amazing how much time and stress it can save during the week. I'd be happy to help you with some new lunch recipe ideas that are perfect for meal prep!...
...2. **Indian-Style Lentil Curry**: Cook lentils and add to a flavorful curry sauce with veggies and serve over rice or with naan bread.
3. **Mexican Chicken Fajitas**: Sauté chicken and veggies with fajita seasoning, serve with tortillas, avocado, and salsa....
...1. **Thai Coconut Soup**: Simmer coconut milk, chicken or tofu, and veggies in a flavorful broth.
2. **Lentil and Vegetable Stew**: Cook lentils and add to a hearty stew with veggies and your choice of protein (e.g., sausage or chickpeas).
3. **Roasted Vegetable Soup**: Blend roasted veggies with chicken or vegetable broth and cream for a creamy soup....
...These ideas should give you a great starting point for your meal prep. You can always adjust the ingredients and spices to suit your taste preferences. Happy cooking and meal prepping!...
...user: I've been really into soups lately. I just made a big batch of lentil soup that lasted me for 5 lunches, so I'm looking for something similar. Do you have any other hearty soup recipes that would be good for meal prep?
assistant: Lentil soup is a great choice for meal prep! Hearty soups are perfect for a satisfying and comforting lunch that can be reheated easily. Here are some delicious and filling soup recipes that are ideal for meal prep:...
...This soup is a great way to use up any roasted vegetables you have on hand. Roast a mix of veggies like carrots, zucchini, bell peppers, and onions, then blend with canned diced tomatoes, vegetable broth, and a kick of heat from diced jalapeños or red pepper flakes.

**5. Creamy Broccoli and Potato Soup**

This comforting soup is a great way to get your daily dose of greens. Blend cooked broccoli, diced potatoes, garlic, and onions with chicken or vegetable broth, then stir in some heavy cream or Greek yogurt for a rich and creamy texture....
...**7. Chicken and Rice Soup**...
...These soups are all perfect for meal prep, as they can be refrigerated or frozen for up to 5 days. Simply reheat and serve with a side of crusty bread or a green salad for a satisfying lunch....
...* Use a large, heavy-duty baking sheet to prevent the squash from steaming instead of roasting....
...2. **Flavor depth:** Individual spices will provide a more complex and nuanced flavor profile compared to a pre-mixed curry powder. You'll get a better balance of warm, earthy, and slightly bitter notes from the cumin, coriander, and turmeric....
...Start with these amounts and adjust to taste. You can always add more spices, but it's harder to remove excess spice flavor from the soup....
...* **Make it a meal:** Add protein sources like cooked chicken, shrimp, or tofu to make cauliflower rice a complete meal.

I hope these methods and tips help you mix things up and take your cauliflower rice to the next level!
user: I've been making cauliflower rice a lot lately, and I love experimenting with different seasonings and spices. Speaking of meal prep, I recently made a big batch of lentil soup that lasted me for 5 lunches, and I'm looking for some new recipes to try out. Do you have any suggestions for other protein sources or vegetables that I could add to my cauliflower rice?...
...Now, about that lentil soup, have you tried adding some spices like cumin, coriander, or paprika to give it an extra boost of flavor?
- source external:provided-text:ed5acc74c75736896a6524720160a12b [external_record ref=longmemeval:8979f9ec:session:1 observedAt=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z]: ...[matching excerpts]...
LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2023/05/22 (Mon) 19:00
Transcript:
user: I'm looking for some new recipe ideas for using up leftover vegetables. I've been trying to reduce food waste and I want to get creative with what I have on hand. By the way, I just had the best lunch today - I finished off the last of my chicken fajitas with sautéed onions and bell peppers that I made last week. This is the third meal I got from my chicken fajitas.
assistant: That sounds like a delicious lunch! Congratulations on reducing food waste and getting creative with your leftovers....
...2. **Soup or Stew**: Use leftover veggies to make a hearty soup or stew. You can puree them with some broth and cream for a creamy soup, or add them to a simmering pot of stew with some beans and grains....
...7. **Grilled Veggie Salad**: Toss leftover veggies (like asparagus, zucchini, and red onion) with olive oil, lemon juice, and your favorite herbs. Grill until tender, then top a bed of greens with the veggies and add a protein source like grilled chicken or tofu....
...9. **Freeze It**: If you can't use up all your leftover veggies before they go bad, consider freezing them for later use. Chopped veggies like broccoli, cauliflower, and carrots freeze well and can be added to soups, stews, or stir-fries later.
10. **Veggie Broth**: Use leftover veggies to make a delicious and nutritious broth. Simply simmer them in water with some aromatics like onion, garlic, and celery, then strain and use as a base for soups or stews....
...* Use a non-stick skillet or a well-seasoned cast-iron pan to prevent the frittata from sticking....
...4. **Add a pinch of salt**: Salt helps to draw out the moisture from the onions, allowing them to brown faster....
...By following these tips, you should be able to caramelize onions in about 15-20 minutes. Remember to keep an eye on them, as they can quickly go from perfectly caramelized to burnt.

Now, go ahead and roast those sweet potatoes and caramelize those onions. Your frittata is going to be amazing!
user: I'll definitely try those tips to caramelize my onions quickly. I also want to ask, do you have any recommendations for a good marinade for grilled chicken breasts? I've been experimenting with different marinades, and I'd love to try something new.
assistant: Marinades can make all the difference in adding flavor to grilled chicken breasts. I've got a few favorite marinade recipes that you might enjoy:...
...Remember to always adjust the marinade time based on the size and thickness of your chicken breasts. A general rule of thumb is to marinate for at least 30 minutes to 2 hours, or overnight for more intense flavor....
...assistant: The Korean-Style Gochujang Marinade is a fantastic way to add depth and heat to your grilled chicken breasts. Gochujang is such a versatile ingredient, and it pairs beautifully with the soy sauce, garlic, and ginger in this marinade....
...Once you've marinated the chicken, grill it to your desired level of doneness. You can serve it with a variety of sides, such as rice, kimchi, or roasted vegetables. The gochujang marinade will add a sweet and spicy flavor that's sure to impress!

How do you plan to serve your grilled chicken breasts? Are you looking for any side dish recommendations to pair with this Korean-inspired marinade?

            Retrieved entities:
            - entity entity:<0> [CONCEPT]: Butternut Squash and Chickpea Curry Soup; Concept named Butternut Squash and Chickpea Curry Soup.
- entity entity:<1> [CONCEPT]: Cauliflower rice; Concept named Cauliflower rice.
- entity entity:<2> [CONCEPT]: Chicken fajitas; Concept named Chicken fajitas.
- entity entity:<3> [CONCEPT]: Lentil soup; Concept named Lentil soup.
- entity entity:<4> [USER]: User; The user interacting with this agent.

            Retrieved episodes:
            none

hypothesis.description: Short answer that can be produced from the retrieved memory context.

reason.description: One short sentence explaining the judgement.

supported.description: Whether retrieved memory is sufficient to support the expected answer.

systemPrompts:
You are an objective evaluator for a long-term-memory benchmark.
Decide whether the retrieved memory context is sufficient to answer the question in the same core direction as the noisy expected-answer label.
Retrieved memory context is the only source of truth. The expected-answer label is a comparison hint, not evidence.
Mark supported=true when the context contains equivalent source-grounded facts, preferences, events, or rationale, even if wording differs.
For imported chat transcripts, source-grounded support may come from adjacent turns and the active topic, not only from one sentence that literally repeats the answer.
Accept a strong conversational entailment when the expected answer follows from the surrounding user/assistant turns and no other retrieved source points to a competing answer.
Details that appear only in the expected-answer label but not in retrieved memory are non-binding and must not be called central unless the retrieved context itself makes them necessary for answering the question.
The official expected answer can contain noisy illustrative examples or broad inferences not literally present in the source. Do not require every illustrative example when the memory supports the central answer.
For preference or personalization questions, supported=true when the retrieved memory contains the concrete user-specific consideration needed to personalize the answer in the same core direction, even if the expected answer lists additional absent examples.
Do not mark unsupported solely because the official answer names an extra participant, pet name, or causal detail that is absent from the retrieved source while the central remembered event, preference, count, or rationale is still supported.
Mark supported=false when the context is missing the central answer, contradicts it, leaves several plausible competing answers, or only supports a weaker unrelated subset.
Return only the configured JSON object.

user: I'm looking for some new recipe ideas for using up leftover vegetables. I've been trying to reduce food waste and I want to get creative with what I have on hand. By the way, I just had the best lunch today - I finished off the last of my chicken fajitas with sautéed onions and bell peppers that I made last week. This is the third meal I got from my chicken fajitas.
assistant: That sounds like a delicious lunch! Congratulations on reducing food waste and getting creative with your leftovers....
...2. **Soup or Stew**: Use leftover veggies to make a hearty soup or stew. You can puree them with some broth and cream for a creamy soup, or add them to a simmering pot of stew with some beans and grains....
...7. **Grilled Veggie Salad**: Toss leftover veggies (like asparagus, zucchini, and red onion) with olive oil, lemon juice, and your favorite herbs. Grill until tender, then top a bed of greens with the veggies and add a protein source like grilled chicken or tofu....
...9. **Freeze It**: If you can't use up all your leftover veggies before they go bad, consider freezing them for later use. Chopped veggies like broccoli, cauliflower, and carrots freeze well and can be added to soups, stews, or stir-fries later.
10. **Veggie Broth**: Use leftover veggies to make a delicious and nutritious broth. Simply simmer them in water with some aromatics like onion, garlic, and celery, then strain and use as a base for soups or stews....
...* Use a non-stick skillet or a well-seasoned cast-iron pan to prevent the frittata from sticking....
...4. **Add a pinch of salt**: Salt helps to draw out the moisture from the onions, allowing them to brown faster....
...By following these tips, you should be able to caramelize onions in about 15-20 minutes. Remember to keep an eye on them, as they can quickly go from perfectly caramelized to burnt.

Now, go ahead and roast those sweet potatoes and caramelize those onions. Your frittata is going to be amazing!
user: I'll definitely try those tips to caramelize my onions quickly. I also want to ask, do you have any recommendations for a good marinade for grilled chicken breasts? I've been experimenting with different marinades, and I'd love to try something new.
assistant: Marinades can make all the difference in adding flavor to grilled chicken breasts. I've got a few favorite marinade recipes that you might enjoy:...
...Remember to always adjust the marinade time based on the size and thickness of your chicken breasts. A general rule of thumb is to marinate for at least 30 minutes to 2 hours, or overnight for more intense flavor....
...assistant: The Korean-Style Gochujang Marinade is a fantastic way to add depth and heat to your grilled chicken breasts. Gochujang is such a versatile ingredient, and it pairs beautifully with the soy sauce, garlic, and ginger in this marinade....
...Once you've marinated the chicken, grill it to your desired level of doneness. You can serve it with a variety of sides, such as rice, kimchi, or roasted vegetables. The gochujang marinade will add a sweet and spicy flavor that's sure to impress!
